title: Does the cyclic group C_8591 admit a (8591,4295,2147)-difference set?
lean_gate_reason: Lean would seal only the auxiliary C_11 theorem slice, not a publishable resolution of the selected row. Formalization is optional archival polish until a C_71/C_121 obstruction or a full construction/nonexistence proof closes (8591,4295,2147).
micro_paper_assessment: The selected row remains a legitimate micro-paper candidate if the exact cyclic row is settled, but the current audited packet is only an exact C_11 theorem slice. It is stronger than an example and structurally real, yet still too far from a standalone short note; preserve it and move on unless a narrow second-quotient obstruction is immediately available.
family_affinity: High affinity with cyclic Hadamard survivor rows where a prime quotient and a contracted multiplier force a tiny orbit-profile corridor.
generalization_signal: Moderate; the quotient-orbit reduction looks reusable, but the lift from a rigid prime-quotient profile to a full theorem is still unresolved.
strongest_honest_claim: Any hypothetical cyclic (8591,4295,2147) difference set has C_11 quotient profile (375;405^5;379^5) or (425;395^5;379^5), up to swapping the two nonzero 3-orbits; equivalently, every order-11 character value is forced to -17 ± 13 sqrt(-11) or 38 ± 8 sqrt(-11).
candidate_theorem_slice: Any hypothetical cyclic (8591,4295,2147) difference set has C_11 quotient profile (375;405^5;379^5) or (425;395^5;379^5), up to swapping the two nonzero 3-orbits.
paper_title_hint: Forced Order-11 Quotient Profiles in the Cyclic Hadamard Case (8591,4295,2147)
next_action: Preserve the exact C_11 slice, cool the row, and do not expand into a broader theorem program from this packet. Reopen only if a tightly scoped C_71 or C_121 obstruction can be attacked within the same one-shot lane.
